title: Xenial Tender Slovakian Tofu Skewers
description: A quick and earthy Slovakian dish featuring marinated silken tofu, perfect for a weeknight dinner or snack. This recipe is Buddhist-temple-friendly and easy to make.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, apple cider vinegar, maple syrup, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the cubed silken tofu to the marinade and toss to coat, making sure each piece is fully covered.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the tofu to marinate.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat, making sure it reaches 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Thread the marinated tofu cubes onto the bamboo skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill or grill pan with a small amount of oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the tofu skewers for 3-4 minutes per side, or until they are golden brown and tender.
Remove the skewers from the heat and serve immediately, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
